heading: Nonliability for financial institutions providing financial records to State child support enforcement agencies in child support cases
label: 669a Nonliability for financial institutions providing financial records to State child support enforcement agencies in child support cases
notes: Amendments1998—Subsec. (a). inserted “, or for disclosing any such record to the Federal Parent Locator Service pursuant to ” before period at end.Pub. L. 105–200section 666(a)(17)(A) of this title
Effective DateFor effective date of section, see section 395(a)–(c) of , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Pub. L. 104–193section 654 of this title
amendments: Amendments1998—Subsec. (a). inserted “, or for disclosing any such record to the Federal Parent Locator Service pursuant to ” before period at end.Pub. L. 105–200section 666(a)(17)(A) of this title
Effective DateFor effective date of section, see section 395(a)–(c) of , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Pub. L. 104–193section 654 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of Federal or State law, a financial institution shall not be liable under any Federal or State law to any person for disclosing any financial record of an individual to a State child support enforcement agency attempting to establish, modify, or enforce a child support obligation of such individual, or for disclosing any such record to the Federal Parent Locator Service pursuant to .section 666(a)(17)(A) of this title

children.heading: Prohibition of disclosure of financial record obtained by State child support enforcement agency
children.label: b Prohibition of disclosure of financial record obtained by State child support enforcement agency
children.text: A State child support enforcement agency which obtains a financial record of an individual from a financial institution pursuant to subsection (a) may disclose such financial record only for the purpose of, and to the extent necessary in, establishing, modifying, or enforcing a child support obligation of such individual.

children.heading: Civil damages for unauthorized disclosure
children.label: c Civil damages for unauthorized disclosure

children.heading: Disclosure by State officer or employee
children.label: 1 Disclosure by State officer or employee
children.text: If any person knowingly, or by reason of negligence, discloses a financial record of an individual in violation of subsection (b), such individual may bring a civil action for damages against such person in a district court of the United States.

children.heading: No liability for good faith but erroneous interpretation
children.label: 2 No liability for good faith but erroneous interpretation
children.text: No liability shall arise under this subsection with respect to any disclosure which results from a good faith, but erroneous, interpretation of subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: $1,000 for each act of unauthorized disclosure of a financial record with respect to which such defendant is found liable; or

children.label: I
children.text: the actual damages sustained by the plaintiff as a result of such unauthorized disclosure; plus

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a willful disclosure or a disclosure which is the result of gross negligence, punitive damages; plus

children.label: B
children.text: the costs (including attorney’s fees) of the action.

children.label: A
children.text: a depository institution, as defined in ;section 1813(c) of title 12

children.label: B
children.text: an institution-affiliated party, as defined in ;section 1813(u) of title 12

children.label: C
children.text: any Federal credit union or State credit union, as defined in , including an institution-affiliated party of such a credit union, as defined in ; andsection 1752 of title 12section 1786(r) of title 12

children.label: D
children.text: any benefit association, insurance company, safe deposit company, money-market mutual fund, or similar entity authorized to do business in the State.

children.heading: Financial record
children.label: 2 Financial record
children.text: The term “financial record” has the meaning given such term in .section 3401 of title 12
